title: Bulk report - Week 23
summary:
Capesize
The Capesize market enjoyed a strong upward trajectory this week, driven by consistent gains in the Pacific and mounting support in both South Brazil and the North Atlantic. The BCI 5TC rose steadily from $19,071 on Monday to $23,572 by week's end, reflecting robust demand and tightening tonnage across both basins. In the Pacific, C5 rates climbed from below $9.00 to $10.40-$10.50 levels, underpinned by limited tonnage, strong iron ore demand, and increased activity from miners and operators.

children.title: Capesize
children.summary: The Capesize market enjoyed a strong upward trajectory this week, driven by consistent gains in the Pacific and mounting support in both South Brazil and the North Atlantic. The BCI 5TC rose steadily from $19,071 on Monday to $23,572 by week's end, reflecting robust demand and tightening tonnage across both basins.

children.title: Panamax
children.summary: The Panamax market wrapped up a notably active week with solid gains, particularly driven by renewed strength in the Atlantic. The North Atlantic led the charge with significant rate improvements, while South America added further support.

children.title: Ultramax/Supramax
children.summary: It was another challenging week for the sector, with rates in both the Atlantic and Pacific regions under continued downward pressure. In the Continent and Mediterranean, sentiment remained largely positional.

children.title: Handysize
children.summary: This week, the market delivered a mixed performance across the regions, with overall sentiment remaining flat. In the Continent and Mediterranean, market fundamentals stayed soft, with limited visible activity.
